qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Improper string escaping in @orval/zod enables generated code injection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets `@orval/zod` string interpolation into `zod.literal(...)` and enum/literal emission in `packages/zod/src/index.ts`, while the advisory targets `x-enumDescriptions` comment emission in `packages/core/src/getters/enum.ts` with pre-fix `jsStringEscape` failing to escape `*` and `/`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described root cause: attacker-controlled `x-enumDescriptions` reaching `packages/core/src/getters/enum.ts` comment generation with pre-fix `jsStringEscape` leaving `*` and `/` unescaped, which allows breaking out of `/** ... */` blocks.
evidence: The advisory fix in `packages/core/src/utils/string.ts` adds `/` and `*` escaping to `jsStringEscape`, and `packages/core/src/getters/enum.ts` uses that helper for `x-enumDescriptions` before embedding descriptions into `/** ${description} */` comments.

The `zod-generated-literal-injection` finding instead describes attacker-controlled `schema.const` and `schema.enum` values flowing into `packages/zod/src/index.ts` `zod.literal(...)` and enum generation, which is a different package, sink, and escaping flaw.
